title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · C Contract Brief: Borrow Lifetime Bound
summary: A media index passes buffers through parsers, caches, and callbacks where no component knows who must release or may mutate them. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem borrow lifetime bound increment in ownership-annotated buffer API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Borrow Lifetime Bound · Theory Borrow Lifetime Bound
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 7614. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
